headline: ManpowerGroup (MAN) Launches IBM Built AI Workflow Tool Through Experis
summary: Experis, a ManpowerGroup (NYSE:MAN) brand, has launched ExcelerateWorkflow, an AI powered workflow solution. The tool is built with IBM watsonx Orchestrate and is designed to move clients from AI experimentation to practical adoption. The announcement highlights a focus on governance and human oversight in real world AI deployments. For investors watching ManpowerGroup, the move into enterprise AI workflow tools sits alongside its core staffing and talent solutions business. Large employers...

headline: Peak Technologies Announces CEO Transition as Company Enters Next Stage of Growth
summary: LITTLETON, Mass., July 09, 2026--Peak Technologies (Peak), a leading global systems integrator and solutions provider, today announced that Joachim (Joe) Heel has been appointed Chief Executive Officer. Backed by Sole Source Capital, Peak continues to invest in its leadership and capabilities as the company enters its next phase of growth, expanding its technology, services, and systems integration capabilities to meet the evolving needs of customers across the retail, manufacturing, warehousing

headline: 1 Services Stock to Target This Week and 2 Facing Challenges
summary: Business services providers use their specialized expertise to help enterprises streamline operations and cut costs. Furthermore, the demand for their offerings is rising as more clients outsource non-core functions, a trend that has enabled the industry to return 15% over the past six months. At the same time, the S&P 500 was up 7.7%.

headline: Software stocks slide as Starbucks builds AI tools to replace vendor software
summary: Investing.com -- IBM, ServiceNow, and Salesforce shares are down in premarket trading on Thursday after a report that Starbucks is developing in-house software using artificial intelligence that could replace applications it currently buys from outside vendors.

headline: Starbucks working on AI tools to replace Microsoft and IBM software - report
summary: Investing.com -- Starbucks Corp. is creating its own software tools using artificial intelligence that could replace applications it currently purchases from Microsoft Corp. and International Business Machines Corp., Bloomberg reported Thursday citing an internal company presentation.

headline: IBM Advances Enterprise AI Software Development with Multi-Agent Capabilities and Specialized Modernization Workflows
summary: Today, IBM (NYSE: IBM) announced major updates to IBM Bob, its agentic software development platform, including new multi-agent capabilities, built-in AI cost and use analytics, and pre-built, specialized workflows for modernizing enterprise systems.

headline: PalmDigitalz Achieves Product Footprint Second Only to IBM in MarketsandMarkets 360Quadrants for Mainframe Modernization, 2026
summary: PalmDigitalz, the Chennai-based mainframe modernization software and services company, has been recognized as a 'Pervasive Player' in the Mainframe Modernization Market by MarketsandMarkets™360Quadrants 2026. with a Product Footprint second only to IBM, placing PalmDigitalz ahead of several long-established global modernization vendors in terms of product breadth and capability.
